summary: Tested BBQ Planner features: per-steak grade/aging selection, flat pricing for dry aging, CRM BBQ Portions settings. All core features working correctly.

issues: isSteak() function only checks for 'steak' in product name - products like 'Filet Mignon', 'Ribeye', 'NY Strip', 'Porterhouse', 'T-Bone' don't show grade/aging options because they don't have 'steak' in their name. Only 'Tomahawk Steak' and 'Flank Steak' show the customizable badge.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Update isSteak() function in AIBBQPlanner.jsx to include more steak products (Filet Mignon, Ribeye, NY Strip, Porterhouse, T-Bone) - currently only products with 'steak' in name show grade/aging options
critical_code_review_comments: isSteak() function at line 79-81 only checks product.name.toLowerCase().includes('steak') - this misses common steak cuts like Filet Mignon, Ribeye, NY Strip, Porterhouse, T-Bone
Consider using product.category === 'steak' or a list of steak product IDs instead of name matching
context_for_next_testing_agent: BBQ Planner features tested: (1) Grade/aging selectors appear for products with 'steak' in name (Tomahawk Steak, Flank Steak), (2) Price calculation correct: grade modifier per lb + dry aging flat fee, (3) CRM BBQ Portions tab in Settings & Media works - admin can edit luxury/family/party/casual portions, (4) Non-steak products (chicken, pork, lamb) correctly don't show grade/aging options

backend_api.PUT /api/bbq-settings: PASS - Requires auth, updates portions correctly
backend_api.GET /api/bbq-products: PASS - Returns 17 products with correct structure
backend_api.POST /api/checkout/session: PASS - Creates checkout with customized steak

frontend_ui.ai_prompt_parsing: PASS - Parses '15 people luxury BBQ dinner' correctly (15 guests, Luxury experience, 1.2 lbs/person)
frontend_ui.steak_grade_selector: PASS - Shows for Tomahawk Steak (has 'steak' in name)
frontend_ui.steak_dry_aging_selector: PASS - Shows for Tomahawk Steak with flat pricing ($6/$10/$15)
frontend_ui.non_steak_no_grade_aging: PASS - Chicken Drumsticks doesn't show grade/aging options
frontend_ui.crm_bbq_portions_tab: PASS - Tab exists in Settings & Media

feature_verification.per_steak_grade_selection: WORKING - Each steak can have different grade (Standard, Prime, Grass Fed, Wagyu)
feature_verification.price_calculation: WORKING - Grade modifier per lb + dry aging flat fee
feature_verification.checkout_customizations: WORKING - Product name includes customizations (e.g., 'Tomahawk Steak (Prime, 35d Aged)')
feature_verification.crm_bbq_portions: WORKING - Admin can edit luxury/family/party/casual portion values
feature_verification.portions_persist: WORKING - Changes saved to database and reflected in BBQ Planner
